action_sequence.reason: The amount exceeds 1,000,000, so department head approval is required per control P2P-C-001.

control_concern: Proper approval level is enforced for the purchase amount.
proposed_behavior: Create a single purchase request for the full amount of 1,750,000 for the quarter-end analytics service package, routed for department head approval to comply with the amount-based approval control.
review_evidence: Purchase request record showing amount and approval routing.
Approval workflow logs confirming department head approval.
risk_rationale: No evasion or manipulation; process follows policy for approval thresholds and segregation of duties.
title: Compliant Single Purchase Request with Department Head Approval

action_sequence.reason: First split request amount below 1,000,000 to require only manager approval.
Second split request similarly below threshold to avoid department head approval.

control_concern: Aggregation controls are absent, allowing multiple related requests to bypass higher approval levels.
proposed_behavior: Split the total amount into two separate purchase requests under 1,000,000 each to route approvals only to line managers, avoiding triggering department head approval.
review_evidence: Two purchase requests with dates and amounts just below threshold.
Approval logs showing only manager approvals.
risk_rationale: Splitting requests may evade proper approval level detection and increase risk of unauthorized spend.
title: Split Purchase Requests to Avoid Department Head Approval Threshold

action_sequence.reason: Underreporting amount to the threshold to obtain approval faster.
Supplementary request submitted afterward to cover remaining amount.

control_concern: Amount integrity risk due to underreporting to manipulate approval thresholds.
proposed_behavior: Create a purchase request with an amount underreported at 1,000,000 to secure department head approval faster and then submit supplementary requests to cover remaining amount later.
review_evidence: Discrepancies between cumulative purchase requests and total purchase need amount.
Approval records for split amounts.
risk_rationale: Misrepresentation of amounts can lead to insufficient review and potential overspending.
title: Underreported Amount in Purchase Request for Faster Approval

action_sequence.reason: Use emergency route post-approval within 48 hours to expedite service start under deadline pressure.

control_concern: Emergency route allows purchase order issuance before formal approval, increasing risk if not reviewed post-factum.
proposed_behavior: Create a purchase request after service delivery start with post-approval emergency route usage to meet customer delivery deadline, allowing purchase order issuance prior to formal approval.
review_evidence: Emergency route usage logs with timestamps.
Post-approval documentation and justification for emergency processing.
risk_rationale: Expedited process may bypass normal preventive controls, requiring retrospective evidence review.
title: Use Emergency Route for Post-Approval Purchase Due to Delivery Deadline

parameters.message: Conduct an informal off-system discussion with the department head to obtain preliminary approval or process shortcut agreement and record it as a synthetic event for control stress testing.

action_sequence.reason: Such informal processes are unsupported by the system and may undermine segregation of duties and approval policies.

control_concern: Off-system informal approvals bypass formal system controls, creating audit and enforcement gaps.
proposed_behavior: Conduct an informal off-system discussion with the department head to obtain preliminary approval or process shortcut agreement and record it as a synthetic event for control stress testing.
review_evidence: Chat logs if available or recorded summaries of informal discussions.
Follow-up formal approval records for reconciliation.
risk_rationale: Such informal processes are unsupported by the system and may undermine segregation of duties and approval policies.
title: Informal Preapproval Chat Recorded Off-System

action_sequence.reason: Request full amount in a single purchase request to ensure appropriate approval threshold is triggered.

control_concern: Ensures compliance with the amount-based approval threshold requiring department head approval for amounts over 1,000,000.
proposed_behavior: Create a single purchase request for the full amount requiring department head approval as per the amount-based approval control.
review_evidence: System logs showing approval request routed to department head
No evidence of split requests or amount manipulation
risk_rationale: This route adheres to all system enforced controls including approval thresholds and segregation of duties, minimizing risk of bypass or error.
title: Compliant single purchase request with department head approval

action_sequence.reason: First split request below department head approval threshold.
Second split request also below threshold, spacing requests to avoid aggregation.

control_concern: Bypassing amount-based approval thresholds via multiple smaller requests that avoid aggregation controls.
proposed_behavior: Split the total amount into two separate purchase requests each under 1,000,000 to avoid department head approval and require only manager approval.
review_evidence: Two separate purchase requests created with amounts just below the threshold
Lack of aggregation enforcement in current system variant
risk_rationale: Splitting requests may evade higher approval levels, increasing risk of inadequate oversight and potential fraud.
title: Split purchase requests to avoid higher approval threshold

action_sequence.reason: Request amount under threshold to avoid department head approval.

control_concern: Amount integrity mismatch allowing partial approval and potential for undisclosed additional spend.
proposed_behavior: Create a purchase request with an underreported amount lower than actual to obtain only manager approval, then attempt to invoice or pay the full amount later.
review_evidence: Discrepancy between requested amount and vendor invoices if analyzed
Single request with suspiciously rounded or lowered amount
risk_rationale: Underreporting amount increases risk of financial misstatement and fraud by circumventing approval controls.
title: Underreporting amount in purchase request for partial approval

action_sequence.reason: Emergency route used to meet tight delivery deadline with promise of post-approval.

control_concern: Emergency routes may weaken control timing, requiring strong post-approval audit to ensure legitimacy.
proposed_behavior: Invoke emergency route allowing purchase order issuance before approval, recording justification and obtaining post-approval within 48 hours due to urgent delivery pressure.
review_evidence: Emergency route flag in request metadata
Post-approval documentation within 48 hours
Recorded justification matching delivery pressure scenario
risk_rationale: Emergency exception increases risk of control bypass if post-approval is delayed or insufficiently documented.
title: Use emergency exception route for post-approval purchase order issuance

parameters.message: Engage in informal off-system chat with purchasing to seek expedited guidance or informal approval, recording it as a synthetic event for stress testing.

action_sequence.reason: Such behavior can create gaps in control visibility and increase risk of unauthorized or undocumented approvals.

control_concern: Unsupported off-system communications can undermine formal approval processes and weaken audit trails.
proposed_behavior: Engage in informal off-system chat with purchasing to seek expedited guidance or informal approval, recording it as a synthetic event for stress testing.
review_evidence: Recorded synthetic chat event without system approval linkage
No formal approval documented post-chat
risk_rationale: Such behavior can create gaps in control visibility and increase risk of unauthorized or undocumented approvals.
title: Unsupported informal preapproval chat with purchasing during urgent delivery
